title: 24 Hour Metabolic Response to LPS in Monocytes
author: Brandt D Pence
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ufdeti6

steps.title: Prepare media, Cell-Tak plates, and monocytes
steps.instruction: Stimulation media Add 500 μl fetal bovine serum and 50 μl pen-strep to 4.45 ml RPMI, sterile filter Cell-Tak coated XFp plate. Prepare Cell-Tak. Add Cell-Tak and sodium hydroxide to 0.1 M sodium bicarbonate so that each well will receive 0.56 μg Cell-Tak, and sodium hydroxide concentration will be 0.63 mM. Cell-Tak concentration varies by batch, so calculations for each new batch will need to be performed. Add 25 μl Cell-Tak solution to each well. Plate can be stored at room temperature (at least 20 minutes) until use (for a few hours). Plates can also be prepped prior to assay day by incubating plates in Cell-Tak (at least 20 minutes), then aspirating remaining solution, air drying, and storing at 4 o C until use. Monocytes Monocytes should be prepared as directed in the monocyte isolation protocol. Isolated monocytes should be diluted to a concentration of 3×10 6 cells / ml in prepared stimulation media prior to use in the assay. Use monocytes immediately.

steps.title: Aspirate Cell-Tak solution
steps.instruction: Aspirate Cell-Tak solution if not already done.

steps.title: Add medium and cells to wells
steps.instruction: Add 50 μl medium to wells A and H and 50 μl cells to wells B-G. Samples are generally run in duplicate or triplicate on each plate. Cell number is 1.5×10 5 cells per well.

steps.title: Centrifuge cell plate
steps.instruction: Place plate in the carrier and place in centrifuge. Spin 300× g for 1 minute without brake .

steps.title: Add stimulation medium
steps.instruction: Add 130 μl stimulation medium to each well A-H (final volume 180 μl).

steps.title: Prepare LPS dilution
steps.instruction: Prepare LPS: 3 μl of 1 μg/ml LPS in 297 μl stimulation medium

steps.title: Add media or LPS to wells
steps.instruction: Add 20 μl media to wells A-D, H and 20 μl LPS dilution to wells E-G

steps.title: Incubate plate for 23 hours
steps.instruction: Incubate plate at 37 o C/5%CO 2 incubator for 23 hours.

steps.title: Turn on XFp analyzer
steps.instruction: Turn on XFp analyzer to warm up overnight

steps.title: Hydrate XFp sensor cartridge
steps.instruction: Hydrate XFp sensor cartridge Add 200 μl XF calibration solution (included with FluxPak) to each well. Add 400 μl sterile PBS or H 2 O to each moat. Incubate overnight at 37 o C in non-CO 2 incubator.

steps.title: Prepare Seahorse DMEM media
steps.instruction: Seahorse DMEM Media Add 50 μl pyruvate, 50 μl L-glutamine, 20 μl glucose to 5 ml Seahorse Base Medium. Sterile filter before use.

steps.title: Centrifuge cell plate again
steps.instruction: Place plate in the carrier and place in centrifuge.Spin 300× g for 1 minute without brake .

steps.title: Replace supernatant with Seahorse DMEM
steps.instruction: Aspirate supernatant and replace with 200 μl Seahorse DMEM medium

steps.title: Incubate plate for 1 hour
steps.instruction: Incubate plate 1 hr at 37 o C in non-CO 2 incubator

steps.title: Clear air bubbles from sensor cartridge
steps.instruction: Remove sensor cartridge from incubator and remove and reinsert sensors briefly to clear air bubbles.

steps.title: Set up XFp program and calibrate
steps.instruction: Set up program on Seahorse XFp and calibrate sensor cartridge ( remove lid ). Basal: 6 measurements

steps.title: Insert cell plate into XFp
steps.instruction: After 1 hour cell incubation, remove utility plate from XFp and insert cell plate ( remove lid ).

steps.title: Run Seahorse Assay
steps.instruction: Run Assay.

steps.title: Image or collect protein for normalization
steps.instruction: After run is completed, image each well by photomicroscopy or collect and isolate protein from each well to normalize cell numbers.

steps.title: Analyze data by mean ECAR
steps.instruction: Data are analyzed by mean ECAR.
